title: Support SSH jumping/bastion hosts for remote access OR local unix sockets
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I have a host that is not accessible from outside networks. With ssh(1), I can use the `-J`/`ProxyJump` option to go through a bastion host and connect to the isolated host from there.
I can also use that in combination with the `-L` option to forward the podman socket to a Unix domain socket on my local machine.
However, neither of these is compatible with podman, as it only supports direct SSH connections. The latter option could theoretically work but, to the best of my knowledge (and according to what I've seen in the man page podman-system-connection-add(1) and the corresponding code), requires a loopback SSH connection, which in turn would require a local sshd, which I do not have on my machine and feels like a significant overhead to set up just for that.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The golang.org/x/crypto/ssh package offers all tools necessary to support this natively: [ssh.Client.Dial](https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/crypto/ssh#Client.Dial) -> [ssh.NewClientConn](https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/crypto/ssh#NewClientConn) -> [ssh.NewClient](https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/crypto/ssh#NewClientConn).
However, having the option to use local Unix domain sockets without requiring loopback SSH could help support other, more generic use cases.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version  
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Git Commit:   9f6d6ba0b314d86521b66183c9ce48eaa2da1de2
Built:        Sat Sep 26 17:50:37 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  memFree: 2764709888

  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: Unknown

    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc92

  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      slirp4netns version 1.1.6

      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.4.2
  swapFree: 2147479552
  swapTotal: 2147479552
  uptime: 2h 51m 5.99s (Approximately 0.08 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ pacman -Q podman

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Local: Bare metal.
Remote: Generic VM.

comments.body: Okay, having done some further experimentation, it appears that, while `podman system connection` does _NOT_ support local sockets (just giving a path works for `add`, but trying to use the connection attempts to establish a loopback SSH connection, which expectedly fails with "connection refused"), setting the  `CONTAINER_HOST` environment variable to `unix://path/to/forwarded/podman.sock` works.
@FallenWarrior2k Interested in creating a PR to fix the system connection if possible, or just documenting the CONTAINER_HOST.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ashley-cui Did you ever get a chance to look at this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Brent now that you are taking on the MAC work, this one seems to fall under that perview.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@FallenWarrior2k Use tcp://<ip>:<port> as the server and connection URL. Note this is an unsecured connection.
